_meta.spike: recipe URL scraping (re-framed from ingredient canonicalization)
_meta.user_review: Final list after user spot-checks. Several original picks 404'd and were replaced with known-live URLs; 3 non-standard placeholders (Reddit / Substack / Medium) were dropped entirely. Pinterest + Instagram retained intentionally as failure-mode tests.

dropped_slots.reason: Discussion thread, not a single recipe entity. Doesn't fit the spike's 'extract one recipe from one URL' frame.
Dropped per user request — Substack recipe posts are functionally similar to Medium (prose-only, no schema). Slot 18's Medium pick covered the same surface; user opted to skip both.
Dropped per user request. Same rationale as the Substack drop.

notes: Pinterest and Instagram remain in the test set as DELIBERATE failure-mode tests. They are NOT bugs; the spike is meant to confirm that even Claude has no useful path on these URL types (Pinterest pins point elsewhere; Instagram caption may or may not contain a recipe). Their inclusion stress-tests the 'expected_to_work_with: [claude_api]' classification.

Slot 7 (Half Baked Harvest) is a deliberate EDGE CASE: a roundup post listing 40 dinner recipes, NOT a single Recipe schema entity. schema.org and recipe-scrapers will both fail because there is no Recipe schema on the page. Claude might extract one of the listed recipes, or might fail gracefully — the spike measures this.

urls.notes: Recipe ID 10813 stable since the early 2000s. Comprehensive schema.org/Recipe markup. recipe-scrapers explicitly supports allrecipes.com.
Ina Garten classic. Embedded JSON-LD. recipe-scrapers supports foodnetwork.com.
Kenji López-Alt's Food Lab cookies. Serious Eats has been a recipe-scrapers reference site for years.

Replaces the original Simply Recipes banana bread (404'd). 40 Aprons is a standard WordPress food blog with Tasty Recipes or WP Recipe Maker — schema.org expected. Reclassified from major_site to food_blog: it's a popular indie blog, not a tentpole site. Provided by user as a known-live URL.
King Arthur Baking flagship site. Strong structured data. Stable URL slug.
Deb Perelman / Smitten Kitchen. WordPress + recipe plugin → schema.org. recipe-scrapers supports smittenkitchen.com.

DELIBERATE EDGE CASE: This is a roundup post listing 40 recipes, NOT a single Recipe schema entity. schema.org will return nothing (no Recipe object on the page) and recipe-scrapers will fail with 'no recipe found' or similar. Only Claude has any path here — and even Claude has to choose: pick one recipe from the list? Extract all 40? Refuse? The spike measures graceful-failure behavior. User-provided URL replacing the original 404'd Tuscan chicken pasta.
Sally's Baking Addiction is one of the most-trafficked baking blogs. Uses Tasty Recipes plugin (schema.org compliant).
Pinch of Yum lentil soup. recipe-scrapers supports pinchofyum.com.

Replaces the original chocolate chip cookies (404'd). User confirmed this URL loads. WordPress recipe blog, standard schema.org expected. Note: original URL had a #wprm-recipe-container-60629 anchor — stripped, the main URL is what matters for scraping.
Nagi Maehashi's RecipeTin Eats. Strong recipe schema via WordPress plugins.
Replaces the original meatballs URL (user wanted higher-confidence pick). Skinnytaste uses Tasty Recipes / WP Recipe Maker → schema.org.
Beth Moncel's Budget Bytes. Dragon Noodles is a viral classic from this site. Strong schema.org via Tasty Recipes.
Replaces the original 2019 chicken noodle soup (404'd). Damn Delicious uses date-based URLs. Recent (2025-09) post. WP Recipe Maker → schema.org expected.

DELIBERATE FAILURE-MODE TEST: Pinterest pins are metadata pointing at a source URL — the pin page itself has thumbnail + description but the recipe lives on the linked site. recipe-scrapers won't touch it; schema.org sparse; even Claude likely can't recover the recipe without following the indirection. Inclusion stress-tests the 'just because URL is feedable doesn't mean recipe is extractable' assumption. NOT a bug if all three approaches fail — it's the expected outcome.

DELIBERATE FAILURE-MODE TEST: Instagram public post. recipe-scrapers will not work. schema.org sparse. Claude may attempt to read caption text (which sometimes contains the full recipe in popular food-creator posts) but Instagram requires JS-rendered scraping in many cases. Inclusion confirms the bound on AI's reach with social-media URLs. Like Pinterest, expected to fail; not a bug.
